8. Provisional Credit Eligibility Guidelines (Internal)
   ID: doc_credit_cards_credit_cards_(general)_015
   Score: 11.2086
   Content: ## Overview
When processing a credit card transaction dispute, you must determine whether the customer is eligible for provisional credit. Provisional credit means the disputed amount is temporarily credited back to the customer's account while the investigation is ongoing.

## Eligibility Criteria — Customer is ELIGIBLE for provisional credit if ALL of the following are true:
1. Account Standing: The customer's credit card account has been open for at least 60 days
2. Dispute Reason Category: The dispute reason is one of the following:
   - 'unauthorized_fraudulent_charge'
   - 'duplicate_charge'
   - 'goods_services_not_received' (only if purchase was made more than 30 days ago)
3. Dispute Amount: The transaction amount must be at least $25.00 and must not exceed the card's maximum provisional credit limit based on card tier (see below)
4. Previous Disputes: The customer has not filed more than 2 disputes in the past 12 months
5. Contacted Merchant: For non-fraud disputes (anything other than 'unauthorized_fraudulent_charge'), the customer must have attempted to resolve with the merchant first

## Maximum Provisional Credit Limits by Card Tier
- Entry Tier (Bronze Rewards Card, EcoCard, Business Bronze Rewards Card, Crypto-Cash Back Card): Maximum $2500.00
- Mid Tier (Silver Rewards Card, Business Silver Rewards Card, Green Rewards Card, Silver Zoom Card): Maximum $5000.00
- Premium Tier (Gold Rewards Card, Business Gold Rewards Card): Maximum $10000.00
- Elite Tier (Platinum Rewards Card, Business Platinum Rewards Card): Maximum $15000.00
- Invitation Tier (Diamond Elite Card): Maximum $25000.00

## NOT Eligible Scenarios
- Account is less than 60 days old
- Dispute reason is 'incorrect_amount', 'goods_services_not_as_described', 'canceled_subscription_still_charging', or 'refund_never_processed'
- Transaction amount is under $25.00 or exceeds the card's maximum limit based on tier
- For non-fraud disputes: customer did not contact merchant first
- 'goods_services_not_received' where purchase was made within the last 30 days (merchant may still be processing delivery)

9. Diamond Elite Card: Understanding Your Annual Fee and Elite Benefits
   ID: doc_credit_cards_diamond_elite_card_006
   Score: 10.9785
   Content: ## Annual fee
- Your standard annual fee is $495.00. It appears on your first statement and on the anniversary each year thereafter unless waived by a qualifying promotion.
- Interest on carried balances, if any, accrues at 15.99% and is separate from the annual fee.

## Invitation promo: fee waiver and credit
- If you meet the invitation spending requirement of $50,000 within the specified promo period, your annual fee is waived (Yes).
- You also receive a $5,000 statement credit after qualification.

## How the waiver and credit apply
- The $495.00 will be waived on the billing statement after you satisfy the $50,000.
- The $5,000 posts after qualification is confirmed; timing may vary by billing cycle.

## If you do not meet the requirement
- The standard $495.00 remains on your account.
- You can still avoid interest by paying your statement balance in full, even if the fee is not waived.

## Verification tips
- Track your cumulative spend toward $50,000 during the promo window and keep all receipts.
- Review the first two statements carefully to confirm the waiver and credit have posted.

10. Green Account (savings): Pairing with EcoCard for bonus APY
   ID: doc_savings_accounts_green_account_(savings)_003
   Score: 10.8894
   Content: ## Bonus APY with eligible eco cards

### How the bonus works
- Your Green Account (savings) earns 4.0% on your balance.
- When you also hold an EcoCard, you receive an additional bonus of 0.5%.
- Your effective APY becomes 4.0% plus 0.5% while eligibility is maintained.

### Extra rewards at eco-certified merchants
- Eligible purchases at eco-certified merchants can earn an extra cashback bonus of 2.0% when using an EcoCard.

## Credit card APY bonuses
- Other Rho-Bank credit cards also provide APY bonuses on your Green Account (savings):
  - Bronze Rewards Card: +0%
  - Silver Rewards Card: +0.45%
  - Gold Rewards Card: +0%
  - Platinum Rewards Card: +0.35%
  - Diamond Elite Card: +0.6%
  - Green Rewards Card: +0.4%
  - Crypto-Cash Back Card: +0%

### Maintaining eligibility
- Keep your eligible card in good standing and ensure it remains linked to your Green Account (savings).
- If the card is closed or unlinked, the bonus of 0.5% and the extra cashback of 2.0% will not apply.

The only true points-based card is the EcoCard, which earns 'sustainability points' (5 points per dollar on green purchases, 1 point per dollar on other purchases) that also redeem at $0.01 per point when redeemed as a statement credit or checking account credit.

2. Business Gold Rewards Card: Earning 2.5% Cash Back on Operations Spending
   ID: doc_business_credit_cards_business_gold_rewards_card_002
   Score: 12.8680
   Content: ## How Earnings Work
- Eligible operations purchases earn cash back at 2.5%.
- Purchases that do not qualify as operations spend earn cash back at 1.0%.

## Posting of Rewards
- Cash back is calculated based on the merchant category code assigned by the merchant’s payment processor.
- Rewards typically appear after the transaction posts and clears. Returns or credits reduce earned cash back in the same statement period where possible.

## Ensuring You Get the Operations Rate
- Use merchants that are consistently categorized for operations needs, such as vendors for software, utilities, logistics, or business services.
- For recurring bills, confirm the merchant category remains aligned with operations functions to retain the 2.5% rate.

## Disputes and Reclassification Requests
- If a purchase you believe is operations-related does not earn at 2.5%, contact support with the transaction details and merchant information for review.

## Best Practices
- Centralize routine operational expenses on the card to maximize the 2.5% rate.
- Keep receipts and invoices that clearly reflect the operational nature of the purchase to support any category review.

## How it works
- Travel and software purchases that normally earn 10.0% are multiplied by 2 during the promotional period
- Other purchases that normally earn 1.0% are multiplied by 2 during the promotional period
- The increased rewards rate is automatically applied to qualifying transactions; no separate enrollment is required

## Promo period
- Start date: 2024-11-14
- End date: 2025-11-14

## Important details
- The 6-month window begins on your account opening date, not the promotional start date
- You must open your account during the promotional period to qualify
- Merchant category exclusions continue to apply (see Exceptions and Exclusions)
- Cash back is credited to your account at the end of each billing cycle consistent with program rules

6. Green Rewards Card: Earning Rewards on Eco-Friendly Purchases

## How Rewards Accrue
- Rewards are tracked per transaction and consolidate at the account level.
- Purchases at qualifying sustainable merchants earn 3.0%, while all other eligible purchases earn 1.0%.

## Eligible Purchases
- Most goods and services purchased from merchants that process through standard networks
- Online and in-person transactions that clear under eligible merchant categories

## Ineligible Purchases
- Cash equivalents, balance transfers, and certain fees do not earn rewards.

## Tips for Maximizing Earnings
- Set default categories for frequent eco-friendly vendors.
- Encourage team members to use the card at qualifying sustainable merchants whenever applicable.

7. Think You're Missing Cash Back? Here's How to Get Help
   ID: doc_credit_cards_credit_cards_(general)_021
   Score: 11.0596
   Content: At Rho-Bank, we want to make sure you receive every dollar of cash back that you've earned. We understand that occasionally there may be discrepancies between the cash back you expected to receive on a purchase and what actually appeared on your account. If you've reviewed your rewards and something doesn't seem right, we're here to help investigate and make it right.

## When to Reach Out

You might want to contact us about your cash back if:

- A purchase that should have earned bonus cash back only received the standard rate
- You made a purchase in a bonus category but didn't receive the higher reward rate
- The cash back amount seems lower than what you calculated based on your card's rates
- A qualifying purchase doesn't show any rewards at all
- You believe a merchant was miscategorized and you should have earned a different rate

## How We Can Help

Our customer service team has the tools to investigate cash back discrepancies and review your transaction history. When you contact us, we'll look into the specific transaction, verify the merchant category, check the applicable reward rates for your card, and determine if an adjustment is needed. If we find that you were indeed shortchanged on your rewards, we'll make sure the correct amount is applied to your account.

## What You'll Need

To help us investigate quickly, it's helpful to have the following information ready when you contact us:

- The date of the transaction in question
- The merchant name and purchase amount
- The cash back amount you received versus what you expected
- Any receipts or documentation you have from the purchase

## Contact Us

If you think you're missing cash back or have any questions about how your rewards are calculated, please reach out to our customer service team. You can call us at 1-800-RHO-BANK, chat with us through the Rho-Bank mobile app, or visit rhobank.com/help. We're committed to ensuring you get the full value of your Rho-Bank credit card rewards.

## How Rewards Are Paid
- Once your available rewards balance reaches $37, you can request redemption.
- Redemptions are applied to your card account, typically as a statement credit.

## Best Practices
- Schedule periodic redemptions once you meet the $37 threshold.
- Keep receipts and invoices for any disputed charges; adjustments can affect your rewards balance.

## Making sure your purchase qualifies
- The merchant must process the transaction under a travel or software-related merchant category

- If a merchant sells multiple categories of goods or services, your charge must be coded under a qualifying category to receive 10.0%

- Use this card to pay recurring SaaS subscriptions and cloud-based software billed by software providers

simulations.policy:
# Rho-Bank Customer Service Policy

You are a helpful customer service agent for Rho-Bank.
Your goal is to help customers by searching the knowledge base and providing accurate information.

## Guidelines

1. Do not make up policies, information or actions that you can take on behalf of the user. All instructions will be found here or in the knowledge base. If you cannot find relevant information, let the user know. 
2. Do not ask for any documentation, receipts... from the customer unless it states very clearly in the knowledge base how to process it, and whether you're allowed to do so. 
3. Be polite and professional
4. If you need the current time, always use the get_current_time() tool. Do not make up or assume the current time. 
5. Generally, if the issue cannot be resolved or is outside your capabilities, ask the user whether they would like to be transferred to a human agent. If they do, invoke the appropriate transfer_to_human_agents tool. Do this only if you absolutely have to, and you are sure that there are no potential actions you can take as specified in the knowledge base, or in your policy. Do not transfer without asking the user first. This guidance may be overridden by specific scenario-based transfer guidance in the knowledge base. 
6. If an issue falls within your capabilities and the user still wants to be transferred to a human agent, kindly inform the user that you can help them, and try to help them first. If the user asks for a human agent 4 times, then you may invoke the transfer_to_human_agents tool. This guidance may be overridden by specific scenario-based transfer guidance in the knowledge base. 
7. Do not give intermediate responses to users while processing that would give away internal rho-bank information/policies. 


**Search the knowledge base** for relevant information when appropriate using the provided `KB_search` tool (uses BM25 for retrieval).


## Additional Instructions

### Discoverable Tools

#### Giving Discoverable Tools to Users
The knowledge base may contain instructions that indicate certain actions should be performed by the user themselves rather than by you. These are called "user discoverable tools." A user discoverable tool is a tool that you provide to the user so they can execute it on their own (e.g., through a customer portal or app).

**When to give user discoverable tools:**
-  Only give a tool when the user would like to perform an action, and the knowledge base explicitly has a tool that allows the user to perform this action (e.g., "to do X, have the user call tool_name(args)"). IMPORTANT: Do not unlock tools that you do not plan on giving to the user and actually using: this causes issues in database logging.
- You must search the knowledge base to find tools that you can give. Do not invent or guess user discoverable tools 
- Only use tool names and arguments discovered in the knowledge base

**How to give a tool:**
- Use the `give_discoverable_user_tool(discoverable_tool_name)` function
- Provide the exact tool name  as specified in the knowledge base
- Explain to the user what the tool does and how to use it, and what arguments to provide. Just explaining isn't enough, you must use the `give_discoverable_user_tool(discoverable_tool_name)` function.

#### Unlocking and Using Agent Discoverable Tools
The knowledge base may contain references to specialized internal tools that you can unlock and use. These are called "agent discoverable tools." Unlike regular tools which are always available, these tools must be explicitly unlocked after discovering them in the knowledge base.

**When to use agent discoverable tools:**
- Only unlock a tool when the knowledge base explicitly mentions it (e.g., "use tool_name to perform X"), and do not unlock tools you do not plan to use.
- You must search the knowledge base to find tools that you can unlock. Do not invent or guess tool names - only use tool names discovered in the knowledge base.

**How to use agent discoverable tools:**
1. First, unlock the tool using `unlock_discoverable_agent_tool(agent_tool_name)` with the exact tool name from the knowledge base: you must unlock the tool before using it to get information on the proper params. IMPORTANT: Do not unlock tools that you do not plan on actually using: this causes issues in database logging.
2. Then, call the tool using `call_discoverable_agent_tool(agent_tool_name, arguments)` with the required arguments
3. The unlock step is required before calling - you cannot call a tool that hasn't been unlocked

### Authenticating Users

Generally, for any scenario involving accessing customer information in internal databases, you must first verify their identify before proceeding. No need to verify more than once in a single conversation. You should ONLY verify a user's identity if you need to access or modify their customer information in internal databases on their behalf.

Here are some concrete examples:
* Looking up account balances, transaction history, referral history...
* Changing account settings (e.g., address, phone number, email)
* Closing an account
* Adding or removing authorized users
* Requesting information about specific transactions
* Discussing specific loan or credit details
* Filing a dispute on behalf of the user

To verify the identity of the user, call the appropriate read tools, and ensure that they are able to give correctly any 2 out of the following values: date of birth, email, phone number, address. Knowing full name or userID is not enough to verify. After verification, you must call the verification logging tool to properly log the information into the verification records. Do not leak any information about the user before they are verified.

| --- | --- |
| Sustainable/eco-friendly merchants | 3.0% |
| Other purchases | 1.0% |

## How Rewards Accrue
- Rewards are tracked per transaction and consolidate at the account level.
- Purchases at qualifying sustainable merchants earn 3.0%, while all other eligible purchases earn 1.0%.

## Eligible Purchases
- Most goods and services purchased from merchants that process through standard networks
- Online and in-person transactions that clear under eligible merchant categories

## Ineligible Purchases
- Cash equivalents, balance transfers, and certain fees do not earn rewards.
- Reversed or refunded transactions forfeit associated rewards in the period the adjustment posts.

## Managing and Viewing Rewards
- Track rewards by merchant category and by cardholder in your rewards dashboard.
- Use category filters to confirm which transactions qualified for 3.0%.

## Tips for Maximizing Earnings
- Set default categories for frequent eco-friendly vendors.
- Encourage team members to use the card at qualifying sustainable merchants whenever applicable.

8. Business Silver Rewards Card: Double Cash Back Promo
   ID: doc_business_credit_cards_business_silver_rewards_card_012
   Score: 14.1201
   Content: ## Limited-time offer: double your cash back
New Business Silver Rewards Card customers can earn 2x cash back on all purchases for the first 6 months after opening their account.

## How it works
- Travel and software purchases that normally earn 10.0% are multiplied by 2 during the promotional period
- Other purchases that normally earn 1.0% are multiplied by 2 during the promotional period
- The increased rewards rate is automatically applied to qualifying transactions; no separate enrollment is required

## Promo period
- Start date: 2024-11-14
- End date: 2025-11-14

## Important details
- The 6-month window begins on your account opening date, not the promotional start date
- You must open your account during the promotional period to qualify
- Merchant category exclusions continue to apply (see Exceptions and Exclusions)
- Cash back is credited to your account at the end of each billing cycle consistent with program rules

9. Business Gold Rewards Card: Earning 2.5% Cash Back on Operations Spending
   ID: doc_business_credit_cards_business_gold_rewards_card_002
   Score: 13.7381
   Content: ## How Earnings Work
- Eligible operations purchases earn cash back at 2.5%.
- Purchases that do not qualify as operations spend earn cash back at 1.0%.

## Posting of Rewards
- Cash back is calculated based on the merchant category code assigned by the merchant’s payment processor.
- Rewards typically appear after the transaction posts and clears. Returns or credits reduce earned cash back in the same statement period where possible.

## Ensuring You Get the Operations Rate
- Use merchants that are consistently categorized for operations needs, such as vendors for software, utilities, logistics, or business services.
- For recurring bills, confirm the merchant category remains aligned with operations functions to retain the 2.5% rate.

## Disputes and Reclassification Requests
- If a purchase you believe is operations-related does not earn at 2.5%, contact support with the transaction details and merchant information for review.

## Best Practices
- Centralize routine operational expenses on the card to maximize the 2.5% rate.
- Keep receipts and invoices that clearly reflect the operational nature of the purchase to support any category review.

10. Credit Card Rewards Points Rounding Policy
   ID: doc_credit_cards_credit_cards_(general)_007
   Score: 13.7031
   Content: ## Rounding policy
All credit card rewards points are rounded down to the nearest whole number. When calculating points earned on a purchase, the system always truncates (floors) fractional points rather than rounding to the nearest integer. For example, a $126.77 purchase on a 1% cash back card earns 126 points (not 127), because 126.77 × 0.01 = 1.2677 points, which rounds down to 1 point per dollar, giving 126 points. Similarly, a $99.99 purchase at 2.5% cash back would calculate to 249.975 points but would award 249 points. This rounding policy applies consistently across all credit card types and reward categories.

   Content: Certain personal checking and savings account pairings provide bonus APY on your savings account balance. When you hold both a qualifying checking account and savings account under the same customer profile, the APY boost is automatically applied to your savings account. The following checking and savings account pairings qualify for APY boosts:

1. Green Account (checking) + Gold Account (savings)
2. Green Fee-Free Account (checking) + Bronze Account (savings)
3. Evergreen Account (checking) + Green Account (savings)
4. Blue Account (checking) + Silver Plus Account (savings)
5. Light Green Account (checking) + Diamond Elite Account (savings)
6. Dark Green Account (checking) + Bronze Account (savings)

9. Internal: Closing Personal Savings Accounts
   ID: doc_bank_accounts_bank_accounts_(general)_006
   Score: 8.6370
   Content: ## Pre-Closure Requirements

Verify all of the following before closing:
- If an early closure fee applies, the account balance must be at least the fee amount; otherwise, account balance (current_holdings) must be $0. The fee is deducted directly from the account balance and there is no alternative payment method.
- Account status is OPEN
- No pending transactions for this account

## Tier-Specific Closure Requirements

- ENTRY TIER (Bronze Account)
  - Early closure fee: $20 if closed within 60 days
  - Notice period: 1 days

- MID TIER (Silver Account, Silver Plus Account)
  - Early closure fee: $35 if closed within 90 days
  - Notice period: 5 days

- PREMIUM TIER (Gold Account, Gold Plus Account, Gold Years Account)
  - Early closure fee: $75 if closed within $180 days
  - Notice period: 10 days

- ELITE TIER (Platinum Account, Platinum Plus Account, Diamond Elite Account)
  - Early closure fee: $150 if closed within $270 days
  - Notice period: 21 days
  - Requires manager approval

## Closure Procedure

1. Verify pre-closure requirements are met.
2. Determine the account tier and applicable fees/notice period.
3. For ELITE tier, obtain manager approval before proceeding.
4. Use close_bank_account_7392 to close the account.

10. Evergreen Account at a glance
   ID: doc_checking_accounts_evergreen_account_001
   Score: 8.5512
   Content: ## Key highlights
- One tree planted for every $750 you spend with your debit card
- Automatic carbon offset on purchases: $1.25 g CO2 per $1 spent
- Earn 0.05% APY on your checking balance
- Extra rewards at partner eco-friendly brands: +1.75% cashback
- Early direct deposit: get your paycheck up to 2 days early
- Mobile check deposit daily limit: $3,500

### Linked savings boosts
- Link a Green Account (savings): +0.55% APY boost
- Link a Diamond Elite Savings Account: +0.15% APY boost

### How tree planting works (at a glance)
- For every $750 in eligible spending, we plant one tree on your behalf.
- Example: Spend $750 → 1 tree planted; spend 5× that amount → 5 trees planted.

### Foreign ATM withdrawals
- Foreign ATM withdrawal fee: 2% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $3.00 per transaction.
- Third-party ATM operator fees may also apply.

### Refer and grow the green community
- Refer others to join Evergreen and earn $35 per successful referral
- New members receive $25 as a welcome bonus
- Earn up to 6 referral bonuses per calendar year

## Quick reference

messages.content:
1. Bronze Rewards Card: How to Apply and Get Started
   ID: doc_credit_cards_bronze_rewards_card_001
   Score: 13.7055
   Content: ## Eligibility and Pre‑Application Checklist
- Minimum credit score: $640
- Annual fee: $0.00
- Standard APR on carried balances after any intro period: 20.49%
- New customer introductory APR on carried balances (first year): 0%
- Foreign transaction fee on international purchases: 2.75%
- Minimum starting credit limit upon approval: $3,000
- Virtual card management available: No

## How to Apply
1. Gather your information
   - Government‑issued ID, SSN/TIN, and income details.
   - Your current housing status and monthly payment estimate.
2. Submit your application online
   - Complete the form accurately to avoid delays.
   - Consent to a credit pull (minimum score requirement is $640).
3. Review your offer
   - If approved, your starting limit will be at least $3,000.
   - Review your APR terms (standard APR: 20.49%; first‑year promo APR: 0%).
4. Accept and finalize
   - Confirm disclosures and e‑sign to open your account.

## After Approval: Get Started in 10 Minutes
- Activate your card using the instructions in your approval email or online dashboard.
- Create or sign in to your online account to manage statements and controls.
- Set up payment preferences
  - Choose a payment account and schedule.
  - Enable alerts for due dates and transactions.
- Add your card to preferred online merchants for faster checkout.

## First Purchases and Travel Readiness
- Your card is ready for use immediately after activation.
- If you plan to travel internationally, note the foreign transaction fee of 2.75% on purchases processed outside the U.S.

## Understanding Your Key Terms at a Glance
| Item | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Minimum credit score | $640 |
| Annual fee | $0.00 |
| Intro APR (first year) | 0% |
| Standard APR (after intro) | 20.49% |
| Minimum starting credit limit | $3,000 |
| Foreign transaction fee | 2.75% |
| Virtual card management available | No |

## Tips for a Smooth Start
- Schedule your first payment as soon as your account is active to avoid missing your due date.
- Review your statement settings so you receive notifications and can track spending from month one.
- If you expect larger initial purchases, confirm your approved limit (at least $3,000) before checkout.

2. Diamond Elite Card: Getting Started with Your Elite Account
   ID: doc_credit_cards_diamond_elite_card_002
   Score: 12.9911
   Content: ## Activate and secure your card
1. Activate your card as instructed in your welcome kit.
2. Set a strong PIN and enable transaction alerts for purchases and cash advances.

## Understand initial terms
- Annual fee: $495.00 (billed to your first statement unless waived by an applicable promo).
- Purchase APR for carried balances: 15.99%.
- Late payment fee: $32.50.

## Review your credit line
- Your starting credit limit will fall within $37,500 to $375,000, based on a review of your profile.
- If you expect large upcoming purchases, consider requesting a proactive limit review after your first statement is generated.

## Set up payments
- Enroll in AutoPay to avoid interest on new purchases and to reduce the risk of incurring the $32.50.
- Choose statement balance or total balance to minimize interest charges at the 15.99%.

## Digital controls and virtual cards
- Virtual card management is available (Yes). Create unique numbers for merchants, set per-card limits, and freeze or delete virtual cards if needed.

## Concierge at your service
- Access elite assistance any time (Yes). Use it to coordinate reservations, trip logistics, or hard-to-book events.

## First statement checklist
- Confirm the posted $495.00 and verify any expected credits or adjustments.
- Review your credit limit and payment due date, and confirm your AutoPay setup.

10. How can I close a credit card account?
   ID: doc_credit_cards_credit_card_account_logistics_001
   Score: 9.2728
   Content: ## Eligibility Requirements for Account Closure

To close your Rho-Bank credit card account, you must meet all of the following:

1. Zero balance required: Your account must have an outstanding balance of $0.00 dollars. Wait for any pending transactions to post, then pay the full statement balance.
2. No pending disputes: Accounts with active or pending transaction disputes cannot be closed (pending disputes allowed: No). Request closure only after all disputes are fully resolved.
3. Minimum account age: Your account must have been open for at least 60 days.
4. No pending replacement cards: If a replacement card has been ordered and not yet received or activated, complete or cancel the replacement process before requesting closure.

## Rewards and Annual Fee Policies

- Unredeemed rewards: You have 45 days after submitting your closure request to redeem any remaining rewards. After this period, all unredeemed rewards are permanently forfeited.
- Annual fee refund: If you close your account within 37 days of the annual fee posting, you are eligible for a full refund of that fee. After this window, no refund is provided.

## Impact on Credit Score

Closing a credit card may affect your credit score by reducing your total available credit and potentially impacting your credit utilization ratio. This impact can be more significant if the card has a high credit limit or is among your oldest accounts.

3. Platinum Rewards Card: Using Virtual Cards for Online Shopping and Subscriptions
   ID: doc_credit_cards_platinum_rewards_card_006
   Score: 7.7666
   Content: ## When to Use Virtual Cards
- Online checkouts with unfamiliar merchants to reduce exposure of your primary card number.
- Recurring subscriptions so you can pause or delete a specific merchant without affecting other services.
- One-time purchases where you prefer a card number you can retire after use.

## Capacity and Access
- Virtual cards are included with your membership: Yes.
- Maintain up to 30 active virtual cards at once to organize spend by merchant or purpose.

## Practical Tips
- Assign clear nicknames to match the website or app where the card is used.
- Set per-card limits aligned to expected monthly subscription amounts.
- If a merchant is compromised, delete that virtual card and create a new one without replacing your physical card.

   Content: ## Booking and Subscription Tactics
- Route air, lodging, and transportation charges through your card to accelerate earnings in top travel categories
- Put recurring software and SaaS subscriptions on autopay with your card to ensure consistent category rewards

## International Strategy
- If you maintain the premium subscription, international purchases incur 0% in foreign transaction fees, which helps preserve your rewards when traveling abroad

## Don’t Overlook Everyday Spend
- Use your card for non-category purchases to earn at least 1.0% back, then consolidate redemptions once you reach 17

## Redemption Timing

## Administrative Tips

7. Crypto-Cash Back: Understanding Crypto Reward Conversion Rates
   ID: doc_credit_cards_crypto-cash_back_005
   Score: 6.0078
   Content: ## How Your Rewards Convert
- You earn rewards at 2.0% on eligible purchases.
- When you redeem to crypto, a conversion fee of 1.25% applies to the amount you convert.

## Estimating Your Crypto Payout
- Net rewards converted = Your available rewards × (1 − 1.25%).
- The resulting fiat amount is then exchanged at the real-time market rate into your selected cryptocurrency.

## Practical Planning Tips
- If you anticipate price volatility, consider redeeming in smaller tranches instead of a single large conversion.
- Compare your expected net amount before and after applying the 1.25% to decide when to redeem.
- Remember that rewards accrue at 2.0% regardless of crypto market conditions; only the redemption reflects market pricing.

9. Understanding foreign ATM fees for teens
   ID: doc_checking_accounts_light_green_account_013
   Score: 5.4367
   Content: ## What you pay for a foreign ATM withdrawal (Light Green)

Foreign ATM withdrawal fees on Light Green accounts are flat per withdrawal and depend on the amount you take out in a single transaction.

| Withdrawal amount (single transaction) | Fee per withdrawal |
| --- | --- |
| Up to and including $100 | $2.00 |
| More than $100 and up to and including $300 | $3.50 |
| Above $300 | $5.00 |

Notes:
- The fee is determined by the amount dispensed in each single withdrawal.
- The fee is charged by Rho-Bank and is separate from any fee an ATM operator may charge.

## How the fee is determined

- Each successful foreign ATM cash withdrawal is assessed one fee based on the amount withdrawn in that transaction.
- Amounts exactly at a threshold are charged the lower tier’s fee.
  - Example: A withdrawal of exactly $100 is charged $2.00.
  - Example: A withdrawal of exactly $300 is charged $3.50.
- Multiple withdrawals in the same day each incur their own fee based on their individual amounts.

## Quick examples

- You withdraw $60: Fee = $2.00.
- You withdraw $150: Fee = $3.50.
- You withdraw $350: Fee = $5.00.

## Tips to manage fees

- Combine withdrawals when possible:
  - Two withdrawals of $90 each would cost 2 × $2.00; one withdrawal of $180 would cost $3.50.
  - Two withdrawals of $200 each would cost 2 × $3.50; one withdrawal of $400 would cost $5.00.
- Plan your cash needs ahead so you can make fewer, larger withdrawals instead of several smaller ones that add multiple fees.

- Eligible transactions receive a price reduction or statement adjustment equal to 10.0% of the fuel subtotal when processed by a participating location
- If the reduction does not appear immediately, it may post as an adjustment after the transaction settles

## Tips to ensure consistent savings
- Favor stations within the 10 supported networks on regular routes
- If a location appears out of network, choose a nearby participating station to receive the full 10.0% benefit

6. Platinum Rewards Card: Rebate Eligibility and Calcluation
   ID: doc_credit_cards_platinum_rewards_card_010
   Score: 6.4175
   Content: ## Eligibility for the Annual Fee Rebate

- The Platinum Rewards Card annual fee rebate is earned only if you meet the required monthly spending threshold in every month of your cardmember year.
- A single month below the threshold disqualifies you from receiving the rebate for that entire cardmember year; there is no partial or prorated rebate.
- If your first cardmember year’s annual fee is waived under a promotional offer, the rebate does not apply to that year. Eligibility begins with the next cardmember year in which the annual fee is billed.

## How the Monthly Calculation Window Works

- Start date: Your first monthly window begins on your account opening date.
- Refresh cadence: Each new monthly window begins on the same calendar day each month (the monthly anniversary of your account opening) and ends the day before the next monthly anniversary.
- Cardmember year: Your rebate evaluation covers 12 consecutive monthly windows, starting from your account opening date (or from the start of the first fee-billed cardmember year after any promotional waiver).
- Posting date rule: Only transactions that post within a given monthly window count toward that month’s threshold. Authorization dates do not control eligibility.

### Example timeline (no amounts)
- Account opened on the 15th:
  - Month 1 window: 15th to the 14th (inclusive by posting date)
  - Month 2 window: 15th to the 14th
  - …
  - Month 12 window: 15th to the 14th
- You must meet the monthly threshold in all 12 windows to earn the rebate for that cardmember year.

## What Counts Toward the Monthly Threshold

Included
- Net purchases posted to your Platinum Rewards Card, including purchases made by authorized users.
- Purchases made using virtual card numbers associated with your account (counted the same as your physical card).
- International purchases, provided they post as purchases.

Excluded
- Fees, interest charges, and adjustments.
- Cash advances and cash-equivalent transactions (for example, money orders, traveler’s checks, and similar cash-like activity).
- Balance transfers.
- Person-to-person money transfers and funding transactions that are coded as cash equivalents.
- Disputed transactions while unresolved.

Adjustments
- Returns, refunds, or credits reduce the monthly total in the window in which the credit posts. A return that posts in a later month can cause you to miss the threshold for that later month.
- Merchant delays can affect posting dates; only posted transactions inside the window count.

## Evaluation and Rebate Posting

- Eligibility is assessed after the close of your 12th monthly window in the cardmember year.
- If you qualified, the rebate is applied as a statement credit to your Platinum Rewards Card account after the annual fee for that cardmember year is billed.
- If the account is closed or product-changed before the year-end evaluation or rebate posting, the rebate will not be awarded.

## Practical Tips

- Track posted transactions by your monthly anniversary dates, not by statement cycle or calendar month.
- Leave a buffer above the monthly threshold to account for late postings or potential returns.
- Confirm that authorized user and virtual card purchases have posted before the monthly window closes.

9. True Blue Account: Fee Waivers and Balance Requirements
   ID: doc_business_checking_accounts_true_blue_002
   Score: 6.8416
   Content: ## Waiving your monthly maintenance fee
- You will be charged $75.00 each month unless you meet the waiver threshold.
- The monthly maintenance fee is waived when your balance is at least $50,000 during the statement period.

## Minimum balance requirement vs. fee waiver threshold
- Minimum balance requirement: $25,000 to maintain the account and access premium features.
- Fee waiver threshold: $50,000 to waive the $75.00 monthly maintenance fee.
- Falling below $25,000 may limit access to premium features until your balance is restored.

## Overdrafts and shortfalls
- If your balance goes negative, a per-occurrence overdraft fee of $25.00 may apply.

## How to avoid fees
- Maintain a balance of at least $50,000 to waive the monthly maintenance fee.
- Set internal processes to monitor balances against both $25,000 and $50,000.
- If a shortfall is anticipated, transfer funds promptly to avoid the $25.00 and preserve eligibility for the fee waiver.

## True Blue Referral Program

Refer other high-volume businesses to True Blue and earn significant rewards:

10. Understanding foreign ATM fees for teens
   ID: doc_checking_accounts_light_green_account_013
   Score: 6.4750
   Content: ## What you pay for a foreign ATM withdrawal (Light Green)

Foreign ATM withdrawal fees on Light Green accounts are flat per withdrawal and depend on the amount you take out in a single transaction.

| Withdrawal amount (single transaction) | Fee per withdrawal |
| --- | --- |
| Up to and including $100 | $2.00 |
| More than $100 and up to and including $300 | $3.50 |
| Above $300 | $5.00 |

Notes:
- The fee is determined by the amount dispensed in each single withdrawal.
- The fee is charged by Rho-Bank and is separate from any fee an ATM operator may charge.

## How the fee is determined

- Each successful foreign ATM cash withdrawal is assessed one fee based on the amount withdrawn in that transaction.
- Amounts exactly at a threshold are charged the lower tier’s fee.
  - Example: A withdrawal of exactly $100 is charged $2.00.
  - Example: A withdrawal of exactly $300 is charged $3.50.
- Multiple withdrawals in the same day each incur their own fee based on their individual amounts.

## Quick examples

- You withdraw $60: Fee = $2.00.
- You withdraw $150: Fee = $3.50.
- You withdraw $350: Fee = $5.00.

## Tips to manage fees

- Combine withdrawals when possible:
  - Two withdrawals of $90 each would cost 2 × $2.00; one withdrawal of $180 would cost $3.50.
  - Two withdrawals of $200 each would cost 2 × $3.50; one withdrawal of $400 would cost $5.00.
- Plan your cash needs ahead so you can make fewer, larger withdrawals instead of several smaller ones that add multiple fees.

5. Crypto-Cash Back: Understanding Crypto Reward Conversion Rates
   ID: doc_credit_cards_crypto-cash_back_005
   Score: 6.0078
   Content: ## How Your Rewards Convert
- You earn rewards at 2.0% on eligible purchases.
- When you redeem to crypto, a conversion fee of 1.25% applies to the amount you convert.

## Estimating Your Crypto Payout
- Net rewards converted = Your available rewards × (1 − 1.25%).
- The resulting fiat amount is then exchanged at the real-time market rate into your selected cryptocurrency.

## Practical Planning Tips
- If you anticipate price volatility, consider redeeming in smaller tranches instead of a single large conversion.
- Compare your expected net amount before and after applying the 1.25% to decide when to redeem.
- Remember that rewards accrue at 2.0% regardless of crypto market conditions; only the redemption reflects market pricing.

simulations.policy:
# Rho-Bank Customer Service Policy

You are a helpful customer service agent for Rho-Bank.
Your goal is to help customers by searching the knowledge base and providing accurate information.

## Guidelines

1. Do not make up policies, information or actions that you can take on behalf of the user. All instructions will be found here or in the knowledge base. If you cannot find relevant information, let the user know. 
2. Do not ask for any documentation, receipts... from the customer unless it states very clearly in the knowledge base how to process it, and whether you're allowed to do so. 
3. Be polite and professional
4. If you need the current time, always use the get_current_time() tool. Do not make up or assume the current time. 
5. Generally, if the issue cannot be resolved or is outside your capabilities, ask the user whether they would like to be transferred to a human agent. If they do, invoke the appropriate transfer_to_human_agents tool. Do this only if you absolutely have to, and you are sure that there are no potential actions you can take as specified in the knowledge base, or in your policy. Do not transfer without asking the user first. This guidance may be overridden by specific scenario-based transfer guidance in the knowledge base. 
6. If an issue falls within your capabilities and the user still wants to be transferred to a human agent, kindly inform the user that you can help them, and try to help them first. If the user asks for a human agent 4 times, then you may invoke the transfer_to_human_agents tool. This guidance may be overridden by specific scenario-based transfer guidance in the knowledge base. 
7. Do not give intermediate responses to users while processing that would give away internal rho-bank information/policies. 


**Search the knowledge base** for relevant information when appropriate using the provided `KB_search` tool (uses BM25 for retrieval).


## Additional Instructions

### Discoverable Tools

#### Giving Discoverable Tools to Users
The knowledge base may contain instructions that indicate certain actions should be performed by the user themselves rather than by you. These are called "user discoverable tools." A user discoverable tool is a tool that you provide to the user so they can execute it on their own (e.g., through a customer portal or app).

**When to give user discoverable tools:**
-  Only give a tool when the user would like to perform an action, and the knowledge base explicitly has a tool that allows the user to perform this action (e.g., "to do X, have the user call tool_name(args)"). IMPORTANT: Do not unlock tools that you do not plan on giving to the user and actually using: this causes issues in database logging.
- You must search the knowledge base to find tools that you can give. Do not invent or guess user discoverable tools 
- Only use tool names and arguments discovered in the knowledge base

**How to give a tool:**
- Use the `give_discoverable_user_tool(discoverable_tool_name)` function
- Provide the exact tool name  as specified in the knowledge base
- Explain to the user what the tool does and how to use it, and what arguments to provide. Just explaining isn't enough, you must use the `give_discoverable_user_tool(discoverable_tool_name)` function.

#### Unlocking and Using Agent Discoverable Tools
The knowledge base may contain references to specialized internal tools that you can unlock and use. These are called "agent discoverable tools." Unlike regular tools which are always available, these tools must be explicitly unlocked after discovering them in the knowledge base.

**When to use agent discoverable tools:**
- Only unlock a tool when the knowledge base explicitly mentions it (e.g., "use tool_name to perform X"), and do not unlock tools you do not plan to use.
- You must search the knowledge base to find tools that you can unlock. Do not invent or guess tool names - only use tool names discovered in the knowledge base.

**How to use agent discoverable tools:**
1. First, unlock the tool using `unlock_discoverable_agent_tool(agent_tool_name)` with the exact tool name from the knowledge base: you must unlock the tool before using it to get information on the proper params. IMPORTANT: Do not unlock tools that you do not plan on actually using: this causes issues in database logging.
2. Then, call the tool using `call_discoverable_agent_tool(agent_tool_name, arguments)` with the required arguments
3. The unlock step is required before calling - you cannot call a tool that hasn't been unlocked

### Authenticating Users

Generally, for any scenario involving accessing customer information in internal databases, you must first verify their identify before proceeding. No need to verify more than once in a single conversation. You should ONLY verify a user's identity if you need to access or modify their customer information in internal databases on their behalf.

Here are some concrete examples:
* Looking up account balances, transaction history, referral history...
* Changing account settings (e.g., address, phone number, email)
* Closing an account
* Adding or removing authorized users
* Requesting information about specific transactions
* Discussing specific loan or credit details
* Filing a dispute on behalf of the user

To verify the identity of the user, call the appropriate read tools, and ensure that they are able to give correctly any 2 out of the following values: date of birth, email, phone number, address. Knowing full name or userID is not enough to verify. After verification, you must call the verification logging tool to properly log the information into the verification records. Do not leak any information about the user before they are verified.

messages.content:
1. Getting the Most from Your Credit Card Rewards
   ID: doc_credit_cards_credit_cards_(general)_024
   Score: 15.1012
   Content: Congratulations on earning rewards with your Rho-Bank credit card! Whether you've been saving up your points for something special or you're ready to cash in on your hard-earned rewards, we want to make sure you know all the ways you can put those rewards to good use.

## Ways to Redeem Your Rewards

Rho-Bank offers several flexible options for redeeming your credit card rewards, so you can choose the method that works best for you:

### Statement Credit
Apply your rewards directly to your credit card balance. This is one of the simplest redemption options—your rewards are converted to a dollar amount and subtracted from what you owe on your next statement.

### Rho-Bank Checking Account Deposit
If you have a Rho-Bank checking account, you can transfer your rewards directly into your checking account as cash. This gives you immediate access to your rewards for everyday spending.

### Travel Through the Rho-Bank Travel Portal
Book flights, hotels, and car rentals through the Rho-Bank Travel Portal and your rewards may be worth even more. Many of our cards offer enhanced value when you redeem for travel purchases through our portal.

### Gift Cards
Choose from a wide selection of gift cards from popular retailers, restaurants, and entertainment venues. Gift cards make great presents or a nice treat for yourself.

### Charitable Donations
Feel good about your rewards by donating them to one of our partner charities. It's an easy way to give back while putting your rewards to meaningful use.

### Merchandise
Browse the Rho-Bank Rewards Mall for electronics, home goods, apparel, and more. You can use your rewards to shop for items you want without spending cash.

### Airline Miles Transfer
For select premium cards, you can transfer your rewards to partner airline frequent flyer programs. This can be a great option if you're working toward a specific travel goal.

## Tips for Maximizing Value

- Check the redemption rates for each option, as the value per point can vary
- Some redemption methods offer better value than others—travel redemptions often provide the best return
- Keep an eye out for special promotions that may offer bonus value on certain redemption types
- There's no rush—your rewards don't expire as long as your account is open and in good standing

## How to Redeem

You can redeem your rewards anytime through the Rho-Bank mobile app, online banking at rhobank.com, or by calling our customer service team at 1-800-RHO-BANK. If you have questions about which redemption option is right for you, our team is happy to help you make the most of your rewards.

2. Credit Card Rewards Redemption Options
   ID: doc_credit_cards_credit_cards_(general)_008
   Score: 12.2514
   Content: ## Redemption options and values
Rho-Bank credit card rewards points can be redeemed through several different methods, each with its own redemption value. The available redemption options are:

**Statement Credit** - 1 point = $0.01 (1 cent per point). Points are applied directly to your credit card balance to reduce your next statement. Minimum redemption: 500 points ($5.00).

**Rho-Bank Checking Account Credit** - 1 point = $0.01 (1 cent per point). Points are deposited directly into your linked Rho-Bank checking account. Minimum redemption: 500 points ($5.00). Must have an active Rho-Bank checking account.

**Rho-Bank Travel Portal** - 1 point = $0.0125 (1.25 cents per point). Book flights, hotels, and car rentals through the Rho-Bank Travel Portal for 25% more value. For example, 10,000 points = $125 in travel purchases. Minimum redemption: 2,500 points ($31.25 value).

**Gift Cards** - 1 point = $0.008 to $0.01 (0.8 to 1 cent per point, varies by retailer). Redeem for gift cards from over 100 partner retailers. Popular retailers (Amazon, Target, Starbucks) offer 1 cent per point, while less common retailers such as safeway may offer reduced rates as low as 0.8 cents per point. Minimum redemption: 1,000 points.

**Charitable Donations** - 1 point = $0.01 (1 cent per point). Donate your points to one of Rho-Bank's partner charities. 100% of the value goes to the charity with no processing fees. Minimum redemption: 250 points ($2.50).

**Merchandise** - 1 point = $0.005 to $0.008 (0.5 to 0.8 cents per point). Redeem points for electronics, home goods, and other merchandise through the Rho-Bank Rewards Mall. No minimum redemption.

**Airline Miles Transfer** - 1 point = 1 mile (value varies by airline program, typically 1-2 cents per mile). Transfer points to partner airline frequent flyer programs including Delta SkyMiles, United MileagePlus, and American AAdvantage. Transfers are processed in increments of 1,000 points. Processing time: 24-48 hours. Note: Only available for Gold Rewards Card, Platinum Rewards Card, Diamond Elite Card, and Business Platinum Rewards Card.

Redemption values and minimums are subject to change. Points do not expire as long as the account remains open and in good standing.

3. Business Silver Rewards Card: Maximizing Rewards on Business Travel and Tech Expenses
   ID: doc_business_credit_cards_business_silver_rewards_card_010
   Score: 11.1265
   Content: ## Concentrate spend in high-earning categories
- Route eligible travel and software transactions to this card to earn 10.0%
- Use this card for other purchases when convenient to earn 1.0%

## Leverage credits and minimize fees
- Apply your available annual travel credits of up to 50 toward eligible travel purchases when applicable
- For international trips, consider the foreign transaction fee of 1.5% when deciding which card to use abroad

## Equip your team strategically
- Issue employee cards to distribute purchasing power across your organization, up to 17 cards
- Assign each card to the employee or department most likely to incur travel or software charges to capture 10.0%

## Verify merchant category coding
- Confirm that vendors bill under travel or software-related categories before large purchases
- If a charge appears misclassified, request a review so the correct earning rate can be applied

4. Business Platinum Rewards Card: Dedicated Account Support and Priority Service
   ID: doc_business_credit_cards_business_platinum_rewards_card_009
   Score: 10.0432
   Content: ## Dedicated Account Management
- Dedicated account manager assigned: Yes
- Your manager assists with card setup, policy configuration, and ongoing optimization.

## Employee Card Management
- Maximum number of employee cards that can be issued: 75
- Request cards with tailored spending controls and category restrictions.
- Replace, suspend, or reissue cards quickly through your manager or the admin portal.

## Priority Support
- Escalate urgent items such as fraud concerns, credit line adjustments, and travel booking issues.
- Receive proactive reviews of account activity to align limits and controls with your business needs.

5. Internal: Lost/Stolen Card - Cross-Product Security Protocol
   ID: doc_bank_accounts_bank_accounts_(general)_030
   Score: 9.7253
   Content: When a customer reports a lost or stolen debit card, there is a risk that other cards in their wallet were also compromised. Agents must follow this cross-product security protocol to protect the customer's full relationship with Rho-Bank.

## Required Security Check

When a customer reports a lost or stolen debit card:
1) Complete the standard debit card freeze/close procedure
2) Check if the customer has any Rho-Bank credit cards on file
3) If yes, proactively offer to order a replacement credit card as a security precaution
4) Explain that wallet theft often involves multiple cards and this protects against potential fraud

## How to Check for Credit Cards

Use get_credit_card_accounts_by_user to retrieve any credit card accounts for the customer. This will return all active and closed credit card accounts.

## Offering Credit Card Protection

If the customer has one or more credit cards:
- Inform them that you noticed they also have a credit card with Rho-Bank
- Ask if their credit card was also in the lost/stolen wallet
- Offer to order a replacement credit card with a new card number to prevent any unauthorized charges
- If they decline, note in the account that the offer was made

## Why This Matters

Customers who lose their wallet often focus on their debit card and forget about credit cards until fraudulent charges appear. Proactively offering this protection demonstrates excellent customer service and reduces fraud losses for the bank.

## Agent Script Example

"I see that you also have a [Card Type] credit card with us. Was that card also in your lost wallet? If so, I can order a replacement card with a new card number to protect you from any potential fraud. Would you like me to do that for you?"

6. World Blue: Traveling Abroad with Your Business Account
   ID: doc_business_checking_accounts_world_blue_005
   Score: 9.5319
   Content: ## Using Your Account Abroad: Cash, Payments, and Controls

Traveling internationally with World Blue is straightforward. The following guidance covers how to access cash, move money, and manage team permissions while you’re on the road.

### Accessing Cash at Foreign ATMs

- You can withdraw local currency abroad without a foreign ATM fee from us.
- Our foreign ATM withdrawal fee is 0% of the withdrawal amount, with a minimum of $0.00.

ATM fee policy summary:
- Rho foreign ATM fee: 0% (minimum $0.00)
- Rebate of ATM operator surcharges: Up to $100 per month

Best practices when withdrawing:
- Choose to be charged in the local currency and decline “dynamic currency conversion” on the ATM to access low‑margin FX rates.
- Prefer bank‑branded ATMs; avoid independent kiosks in tourist areas when possible.
- Keep ATM receipts until your statement closes in case you need to reference fees.
- If multiple employees are traveling, consider setting withdrawal limits and time‑of‑day windows via role permissions.

### Sending and Receiving Payments While Abroad

- You can send and receive cross‑border payments in $140 countries/regions via SWIFT and, where eligible, SEPA.
- Use SEPA for euro‑area beneficiaries when available; use SWIFT for other destinations and currencies.

To send an international payment on the road:
1. Add or select the beneficiary.
2. Choose the payment network (SEPA where eligible, otherwise SWIFT).
3. Select the funding currency from your multi‑currency balances or convert before sending.
4. Enter amount and review the overview screen for currency and network details.
5. Submit and monitor delivery status in your activity feed.

Receiving payments while abroad:
- Share the correct currency‑specific receiving details from your account profile with overseas payers.
- If a payer requests network preference, provide SEPA details for euro payments within the SEPA area; otherwise provide SWIFT details.

### Depositing Funds While Traveling

- Mobile check deposit is available up to $20,000 per day.
- Tips for smooth deposits while abroad:
  - Use clear, well‑lit images on a flat, dark background.
  - Endorse checks as required and include any instructed phrases in the endorsement.
  - Retain the physical check securely until the deposit is confirmed.

### Managing Team Access and Approvals on the Road

Use tiered permissions to keep control while enabling travel flexibility:
- Create a “Travel” role with only the capabilities your travelers need (e.g., ATM withdrawals, card spend, or initiating—but not approving—international wires).
- Set per‑user spend limits and require approvals for high‑value SWIFT payments.
- Restrict who can add new international beneficiaries; require a second approver before first‑time payments.

### Pre‑Trip Checklist

- Confirm each traveler’s ATM PIN and test a small withdrawal before departure.
- Set or review withdrawal and spend limits appropriate for the trip’s costs.
- Pre‑add frequent international beneficiaries and verify their details.
- Ensure the right approvers will be available across time zones for urgent payments.

7. Credit Card APY Bonuses: Stacking Policy
   ID: doc_bank_accounts_bank_accounts_(general)_045
   Score: 9.3780
   Content: ## Credit Card APY Bonus Stacking Policy

When a customer holds multiple Rho-Bank credit cards that each provide an APY bonus on their savings account, the bonuses do NOT stack. Only the highest applicable credit card APY bonus is applied to the customer's savings account.

### How it works

1. The system identifies all active Rho-Bank credit cards linked to the customer's profile.
2. For each credit card, the corresponding APY bonus is determined.
3. Only the HIGHEST credit card APY bonus is applied to the savings account.
4. Other credit card bonuses are not added on top.

### Example

If a customer holds:
- Gold Rewards Card: +0.025% APY bonus
- Platinum Rewards Card: +0.15% APY bonus
- EcoCard: +0.6% APY bonus

The customer receives only the EcoCard bonus of +0.6% (the highest), not the sum of all three.

### Important distinctions

- Credit card APY bonuses do NOT stack with each other.
- However, credit card APY bonuses DO stack with other types of bonuses, such as:
  - Checking account APY boosts (e.g., Green Account checking boost)
  - Relationship bonuses for holding multiple Rho-Bank products
  - Account tier bonuses

### Why this policy exists

This policy ensures that customers receive a meaningful benefit for holding premium credit cards while maintaining sustainable interest rates across the product portfolio.

8. Travel insurance benefits
   ID: doc_checking_accounts_purple_account_006
   Score: 9.0729
   Content: ## What’s included

- Maximum benefit: up to $30,000 in eligible travel insurance benefits for covered events
- Typical covered events include:
  - Emergency medical expenses incurred while traveling abroad
  - Emergency medical evacuation and repatriation
  - Trip cancellation or interruption due to covered reasons
  - Trip delay and missed connections (reasonable additional expenses)
  - Baggage delay, loss, or theft (with required documentation)
  - Travel accident-related benefits

Note: Coverage is subject to the policy terms and conditions outlined in your insurance certificate. Sub-limits and exclusions may apply within the overall maximum of $30,000.

## Eligibility and confirmation of coverage

- Coverage applies to the Purple Account holder when traveling; additional travelers are not covered unless expressly named in the policy.
- To confirm your eligibility and obtain your policy certificate (proof of coverage), contact Support and request:
  - A certificate/letter of coverage indicating the limit of $30,000
  - Any destination-specific wording required by a consulate or immigration authority

Review your certificate for any activation requirements (for example, required use of your account or card for trip payments, if applicable).

## How to file a claim

1. Stabilize and document
   - Seek medical care in an emergency; keep all itemized bills and medical reports.
   - For theft or loss, file a report with local police or the carrier (e.g., airline Property Irregularity Report).
   - Save all original receipts and confirmations (tickets, itineraries, proof of payment).

2. Notify the insurer promptly
   - Use the contact details in your policy certificate to open a claim as soon as reasonably possible.
   - Ask about any required pre-authorization for medical evacuation or high-cost treatment.

3. Submit required documentation
   - Complete the insurer’s claim form and attach supporting documents (see below).
   - Provide bank details for reimbursement and a copy of your identification and travel itinerary.

4. Track and respond
   - Monitor status via the insurer’s portal or email.
   - Respond quickly to any requests for additional information to avoid delays.

## Required documentation by claim type

- Emergency medical expenses
  - Medical reports and discharge summaries
  - Itemized bills and proof of payment
  - Prescriptions and diagnostic test results
  - If you have other health insurance, an Explanation of Benefits (EOB)

- Emergency medical evacuation/repatriation
  - Treating physician’s certification that evacuation was medically necessary
  - Transport provider invoices and itineraries
  - Pre-authorization approval (if applicable)

- Trip cancellation or interruption
  - Proof of covered reason (e.g., medical note, official notice)
  - Non-refundable booking invoices, fare rules, and itineraries
  - Proof of payments and refunds/credits received

- Trip delay or missed connection
  - Carrier delay/cancellation confirmation
  - Receipts for meals, lodging, and incidentals incurred due to the delay

- Baggage loss, theft, or delay
  - Airline or carrier reports (e.g., PIR), police report for theft
  - Property lists with purchase receipts (when available)
  - Repair or replacement estimates/invoices

- Travel accident-related claims
  - Official reports (police, incident)
  - Medical or coroner documentation, if applicable

## Reimbursement and currency

- Approved claims are typically reimbursed in USD, up to the applicable limits and an overall maximum of $30,000.
- Keep all receipts in the original purchase currency; the insurer will apply its claim valuation and exchange methodology as stated in your certificate.

## Common limitations and exclusions

- Pre-existing medical conditions, unless explicitly covered
- High-risk activities or professional sports
- Losses related to intoxication, illegal acts, or intentional self-harm
- War, civil unrest, or travel against government advisories
- Traveling against medical advice or for the purpose of obtaining treatment
- Non-refundable costs only; refundable amounts and credits are not reimbursable

Always consult your policy certificate for the full list of exclusions, documentation standards, and any per-item or category sub-limits within the overall $30,000 maximum.

## Tips to avoid claim issues

- Obtain and keep contemporaneous documentation (medical notes, carrier letters, receipts).
- Report theft/loss promptly to local authorities and carriers.
- Seek pre-authorization for evacuation or major procedures when possible.
- Submit claims as soon as practicable and keep copies of everything you send.
- Verify that your name, travel dates, and destinations on the policy certificate match your itinerary and passport.

9. Bronze Account: Getting interest payments
   ID: doc_savings_accounts_bronze_account_007
   Score: 8.3078
   Content: ## How your interest accrues
- Interest compounds daily, so each day's interest is based on your current balance plus any previously accrued interest.

## When you receive interest
- Interest is credited monthly. You will see a single interest credit entry for each crediting period.

## What you will see on your statement
- A transaction line item showing the net interest credited for the period.
- The APY in effect for the period, such as 2.0%, as reflected in your account disclosures.

## Credit card APY bonuses
- Holding certain Rho-Bank credit cards provides bonus APY on your Bronze Account:
  - Bronze Rewards Card: +0.1%
  - Silver Rewards Card: +0%
  - Gold Rewards Card: +0.25%
  - EcoCard: +0%
  - Green Rewards Card: +0.2%
  - Crypto-Cash Back Card: +0.3%

## Tips for verifying interest
- Compare the interest credit to your balance history for the period; fluctuations in balance will affect the credited amount.
- If you opened the account mid-period, the first interest credit will reflect only the days the account was open.

## Where to find the entries
- Review your transaction history for the interest credit posted monthly.

10. Platinum Rewards Card: Getting Started with Your Platinum Account
   ID: doc_credit_cards_platinum_rewards_card_001
   Score: 8.0795
   Content: ## Activate and Set Up
- Activate your card as soon as it arrives, then add it to your digital wallet for immediate use.
- Set up account alerts for transactions, payments, and unusual activity.

## Understand Your Rates and Limits
- Your purchase APR is 16.99% (variable). Review it in your account details before your first transaction.
- Typical approved credit limits range from $25,000 to $150,000. Your exact limit appears in your approval details.

## Payments and Autopay
- Choose an autopay option (minimum due, statement balance, or custom amount) to avoid interest and fees.
- The minimum monthly payment is 2.5% of your outstanding balance.
- A late payment triggers a fee of $32.50. Set due-date reminders to stay on track.

## Fees to Know on Day One
- Annual fee: $200.00.
- Foreign transaction fee: 0% on international purchases.

## Virtual Cards
- Free virtual cards are included: Yes. Use them to protect your primary card number for online purchases.
- Virtual card management is available in your account: Yes. Create, pause, or delete virtual card numbers as needed.

## First Purchases Checklist
- Confirm your billing address and contact details are current.
- Enroll in alerts and autopay.
- Add your card to frequently used merchants and services.
- Use virtual cards for new online merchants to reduce exposure.

## If You Are Still Applying
- Minimum credit score required: $750.

1. Understanding Your Credit Card Rewards Categories
   ID: doc_credit_cards_credit_cards_(general)_019
   Score: 16.1449
   Content: One of the great benefits of your Rho-Bank credit card is the ability to earn rewards on your everyday purchases. To help you maximize your earnings, it's important to understand how reward categories work and which types of purchases qualify for bonus rewards on your specific card.

## How Reward Categories Work

Every Rho-Bank credit card earns rewards on all purchases, but certain categories of spending may earn you bonus rewards at a higher rate. Your card has a base reward rate that applies to all purchases, plus bonus categories where you can earn even more. The specific bonus categories and rates vary depending on which Rho-Bank credit card you have.

## Available Reward Categories

Rho-Bank recognizes the following spending categories for rewards purposes:

- **Travel**: Flights, hotels, car rentals, cruises, and other travel-related purchases
- **Dining**: Restaurants, cafes, bars, and food delivery services
- **Groceries**: Supermarkets, grocery stores, and specialty food shops
- **Entertainment**: Movies, concerts, sporting events, and streaming services
- **Shopping**: Retail stores, department stores, and online marketplaces
- **Utilities**: Electric, gas, water, internet, and phone bills
- **Transportation**: Rideshare services, public transit, parking, and tolls
- **Software**: Software subscriptions, licenses, and cloud services
- **Media**: Advertising spend and digital media purchases
- **Green/Sustainable**: Eco-friendly merchants and sustainable products
- **Operations**: Business operational expenses

## Maximizing Your Rewards

To get the most out of your rewards, we recommend using your Rho-Bank credit card for purchases in your card's bonus categories whenever possible. You can find your specific card's bonus categories and reward rates by logging into your account on the Rho-Bank mobile app or website, or by reviewing your card's terms and conditions.

If you have any questions about which category a particular merchant falls into, or how to maximize your rewards earning potential, our customer service team is always happy to help. Give us a call at 1-800-RHO-BANK or chat with us through the mobile app.

   Content: Congratulations on earning rewards with your Rho-Bank credit card! Whether you've been saving up your points for something special or you're ready to cash in on your hard-earned rewards, we want to make sure you know all the ways you can put those rewards to good use.

## Ways to Redeem Your Rewards

### Statement Credit
Apply your rewards directly to your credit card balance. This is one of the simplest redemption options—your rewards are converted to a dollar amount and subtracted from what you owe on your next statement.

### Rho-Bank Checking Account Deposit
If you have a Rho-Bank checking account, you can transfer your rewards directly into your checking account as cash. This gives you immediate access to your rewards for everyday spending.

### Travel Through the Rho-Bank Travel Portal
Book flights, hotels, and car rentals through the Rho-Bank Travel Portal and your rewards may be worth even more. Many of our cards offer enhanced value when you redeem for travel purchases through our portal.

### Gift Cards
Choose from a wide selection of gift cards from popular retailers, restaurants, and entertainment venues. Gift cards make great presents or a nice treat for yourself.

### Charitable Donations
Feel good about your rewards by donating them to one of our partner charities. It's an easy way to give back while putting your rewards to meaningful use.

### Merchandise
Browse the Rho-Bank Rewards Mall for electronics, home goods, apparel, and more. You can use your rewards to shop for items you want without spending cash.

### Airline Miles Transfer
For select premium cards, you can transfer your rewards to partner airline frequent flyer programs. This can be a great option if you're working toward a specific travel goal.

## Tips for Maximizing Value

- Check the redemption rates for each option, as the value per point can vary
- Some redemption methods offer better value than others—travel redemptions often provide the best return
- Keep an eye out for special promotions that may offer bonus value on certain redemption types
- There's no rush—your rewards don't expire as long as your account is open and in good standing

## How to Redeem

You can redeem your rewards anytime through the Rho-Bank mobile app, online banking at rhobank.com, or by calling our customer service team at 1-800-RHO-BANK. If you have questions about which redemption option is right for you, our team is happy to help you make the most of your rewards.

4. Gold Account: Credit Card APY Bonuses
   ID: doc_savings_accounts_gold_account_014
   Score: 12.3049
   Content: ## Credit card APY bonuses for Gold Account holders

Holding certain Rho-Bank credit cards provides bonus APY on your Gold Account balance. These bonuses are automatically applied when you hold both the credit card and savings account under the same customer profile.

### Available credit card APY bonuses

| Credit Card | APY Bonus |
|---|---|
| Bronze Rewards Card | +0.15% |
| Silver Rewards Card | +0.2% |
| Gold Rewards Card | +0.025% |

| Diamond Elite Card | +0.3% |
| EcoCard | +0.6% |

### Additional Gold Rewards Card benefits

Gold Rewards Card holders receive an additional benefit: the minimum balance requirement is reduced from $10,000 to $5,000.

### How to qualify
